candidates.direction_name: Trim live state inside the recovered 128x128 PTX hot-band control path

score_breakdown.reason: Matches the dominant register / occupancy wall on the recovered winner while staying inside one bounded PTX-local code surface.

ranking_notes: Registers Per Thread and Achieved Occupancy are still the highest-value structured findings.
This stays on the recovered winner instead of paying another geometry-switch tax.

candidates.hypothesis: If the recovered PTX winner drops a small amount of register lifetime in the steady-state loop, achieved warps should rise above the current 16.63% plateau without reopening the round-1 256x128 regression.
candidates.expected_bottleneck: occupancy_latency_hiding_issue with a secondary tensor_core_underutilization component on the accepted 128x128 PTX hot-band path

candidates.direction_name: Collapse PTX wait-group and consumer barrier cadence without growing shared state

score_breakdown.reason: Barrier is the clearest secondary stall and this branch stays on the active PTX winner while keeping the same shared-memory budget.

ranking_notes: Barrier is not the primary wall, so this stays below the register / occupancy trim.

candidates.expected_bottleneck: synchronization_barrier_issue with occupancy_latency_hiding_issue as the guardrail

candidates.direction_name: Repair the 256x128 half-panel register-reuse branch with compact B staging

score_breakdown.reason: High-upside geometry family with proven sensitivity to register pressure, but the repair scope is materially larger than the PTX-local options.

ranking_notes: This keeps one non-PTX family live so the queue does not collapse into only one surface.
It stays third because round 1 already showed how easily the wide branch can trade lower registers for worse barrier and short-scoreboard behavior.
Use it only after bounded PTX-local exploits flatten out.
candidates.hypothesis: The 256x128 geometry is still the only alternate family that materially changed register pressure, so a repaired half-panel branch could beat the PTX plateau if compact B staging survives without reintroducing the barrier and short-scoreboard collapse seen in round 1.
candidates.expected_bottleneck: occupancy_latency_hiding_issue on the wide hot-band geometry, with secondary synchronization_barrier_issue and short_scoreboard sensitivity
candidates.stop_condition: Stop immediately if runtime moves back toward the round-1 30 ms regression or if short-scoreboard and barrier both spike together.
